stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking around a significant open-source LLM release.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core NLP/LLM ecosystem, specifically using the 'text-generation' pipeline.
The tags indicate relevance to major frameworks like transformers and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: Tests suggest Russian satellites can jam GPS on a continental scale
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Tests indicate Russian satellites possess the capability to jam GPS signals across a continental scale.
The report links recent, mysterious GPS interference observed across Europe to potential Russian activity.
The story raises significant geopolitical and technological concerns regarding satellite warfare and infrastructure vulnerability.

stories.title: macOS 27 requires Apple Silicon, as Apple draws down the Intel Mac era
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: macOS 27 will mandate Apple Silicon (M1 or newer) for operation.
This signals the definitive end of support for Intel-based Macs in the upcoming OS release.
It marks a significant, ongoing industry shift toward Apple's proprietary silicon ecosystem.

stories.title: Meta alleges NSO violated spyware injunction with new WhatsApp attacks
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Meta (WhatsApp) accuses NSO Group of violating a spyware injunction.
The allegations stem from new spyware attacks targeting WhatsApp users.
WhatsApp is requesting the court to hold NSO in contempt for the alleged violations.

stories.title: Collaborative Human-Agent Protocol (CHAP)
stories.author: Philip Black
bullet_points: Foundation models are evolving beyond simple response generation into complex operational roles.
Future AI deployments involve multi-human, multi-agent collaborations handling critical tasks (e.g., code, clinical decisions).
These advanced systems require protocols for planning, tool-calling, and coordinating across diverse teams and trust boundaries.

stories.title: Microsoft's open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
stories.author: raffael_de
bullet_points: Microsoft's open-source tools were compromised in a security breach.
The attack specifically targeted and resulted in the theft of passwords belonging to AI developers.
This highlights significant security vulnerabilities within widely used developer tooling in the AI space.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI, indicating its primary function.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and performance improvements.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Apple’s AI pitch will live or die by its privacy promise
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Apple heavily featured AI in its recent WWDC keynote.
Apple's core strategy for its AI rollout centers on emphasizing superior user privacy.
The company positioned its measured approach to AI development as a commitment to doing things 'right' from a privacy standpoint.

stories.title: Apple says its AI is still private, even when it's running on Google's servers
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Apple is implementing AI features that run on Google's cloud infrastructure.
A key privacy feature ensures that Google cannot access the data being processed by Apple's AI models.
This signals a trend toward utilizing third-party cloud providers while maintaining user-centric data privacy.

stories.title: Say hi to "Siri AI"—Apple announces new, more "conversational" voice assistant
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Apple is introducing a new, more 'conversational' voice assistant, dubbed 'Siri AI'.
The update will feature significant changes to the AI model, which is being overhauled using Google-powered technology.
These new features are scheduled to roll out to users this fall.

stories.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
stories.author: Ruslan Salakhutdinov
bullet_points: Introduces iOSWorld, a novel benchmark specifically designed for evaluating 'personally intelligent' phone agents.
The benchmark simulates a persistent user identity across 26 native iOS apps, allowing agents to reason over personal data (transactions, messages, etc.).
Addresses a key limitation in existing mobile agent benchmarks by focusing on personalization rather than isolated, impersonal tasks.

stories.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
stories.author: Ruslan Salakhutdinov
bullet_points: Introduces iOSWorld, a novel benchmark designed to test the 'personal intelligence' of phone agents.
The benchmark simulates a persistent user identity across 26 native iOS apps, allowing agents to reason over user history and preferences.
Addresses a gap in existing mobile agent benchmarks by moving beyond impersonal, isolated instruction following.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across diverse hardware setups.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It integrates built-in support for Ollama, a popular local LLM runner.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its capabilities to support the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The update facilitates the use of proprietary/API-based models alongside open-source models.

stories.title: The Rivian R2 is too much fun to let drive itself
stories.author: Lawrence Ulrich
bullet_points: Rivian is heavily invested in robotaxis and autonomous driving technology.
The upcoming 2027 R2 model is positioned as Rivian's key vehicle for achieving mainstream market success.
The company faces the challenge of transitioning from cult-favorite status (R1S/R1T) to broader consumer acceptance.

stories.title: First Drive: The 2027 Rivian R2 entirely changes the EV game
stories.author: Michael Teo Van Runkle
bullet_points: Rivian is launching its second electric vehicle, the R2.
The R2 is positioned as a sub-$60,000 model.
The launch is framed as a significant potential shift in the EV market.

stories.title: FCC lifts looming deadline for Amazon Leo satellite broadband constellation
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The FCC has granted a waiver regarding the deadline for Amazon's Leo satellite broadband constellation.
The waiver's justification cites the public interest served by promoting a second large satellite broadband network.
This development relates to the deployment and regulatory approval of next-generation satellite internet services.

stories.title: iOS 27 and iPadOS 27 don't drop support for any iPhones—and just a few iPads
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: iOS 27 and iPadOS 27 will maintain support for a broad range of existing iPhones.
The update is expected to be beneficial for users on older iPhone models.
Support for iPads will be limited to only a few specific models.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 and Antigravity come to Google NotebookLM
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google NotebookLM is receiving a significant upgrade with the integration of Gemini 3.5 and Antigravity.
The advanced features are currently restricted to users on AI Ultra and enterprise accounts.
This update enhances the capabilities of the AI-powered note-taking and research tool.

stories.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
stories.author: Xiaojuan Qi
bullet_points: Introduces OmniGameArena, a new unified benchmark designed for evaluating Vision-Language Model (VLM) agents in interactive game environments.
Addresses limitations in existing benchmarks, such as reporting only single first-attempt scores and focusing solely on single-agent solo play.
Provides a standardized platform to compare heterogeneous agent classes (commercial, open-weight, and specialized policies) across twelve new Unreal Engine 5 games.

stories.title: Evaluation Cards: An Interpretive Layer for AI Evaluation Reporting
stories.author: Irene Solaiman
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of inconsistent reporting of AI evaluation results across various platforms (leaderboards, model cards, etc.).
Proposes 'Evaluation Cards' as an interpretive layer to standardize and improve the traceability of AI evaluation data.
Aims to solve gaps where current efforts only cover narrow parts of the evaluation lifecycle without composing a single interpretable record.

stories.title: Topological Neural Operators
stories.author: Tolga Birdal
bullet_points: Introduces Topological Neural Operators (TNOs) to advance operator learning on cell complexes.
Lifts Neural Operators from point/edge functions to topological domains, representing data on cells of varying dimensions.
Utilizes Discrete Exterior Calculus to model interactions and enable explicit cross-dimensional coupling (e.g., gradient, curl, divergence).

stories.title: FASE: Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy for Code Quality
stories.author: Ladan Tahvildari
bullet_points: Addresses the reliability issues (LLM hallucinations, error propagation) in multi-agent code generation systems.
Introduces Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy (FASE) to quantify uncertainty in code generation.
Aims to improve upon existing methods by providing a less costly alternative to LLM-driven equivalence checks.

stories.title: Who Earns the Safety? Intervention-Aware Quantum Predictive Control with Safety Attribution
stories.author: Yifan Wang
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical issue in AI safety: relying solely on downstream hard safety filters can mask poor performance in the upstream learned controller.
It proposes a framework to determine 'who earns the safety'—whether the policy itself or its protective layers are responsible for constraint satisfaction.
This research moves beyond simple post-hoc filtering by integrating safety attribution directly into the policy learning process.

stories.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
stories.author: Lianhui Qin
bullet_points: The research addresses the difficulty domain scientists face in configuring advanced scientific simulators.
It frames simulator setup as an 'agent-tool interface grounding' problem for coding agents.
The goal is to determine the minimal, simulator-specific adaptations needed for general coding agents to interact with specialized scientific software.

stories.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
stories.author: Tianyu Pang
bullet_points: The paper addresses challenges in Reinforcement Learning (RL) applied to Large Language Models (LLMs), specifically concerning post-training optimization.
It critiques the use of ratio-clipping mechanisms (like in PPO/GRPO) as an inadequate proxy for distributional shift, especially with long-tailed vocabularies.
The research suggests rethinking divergence regularization techniques to improve the stability and effectiveness of LLM RL.

stories.title: Topological Neural Operators
stories.author: Tolga Birdal
bullet_points: Introduces Topological Neural Operators (TNOs) as a new framework for operator learning on cell complexes.
Extends Neural Operators (NOs) from point/edge functions to topological domains, handling data across varying dimensions.
Utilizes Discrete Exterior Calculus to model interactions and enable explicit cross-dimensional coupling (e.g., gradient, curl, divergence).

stories.title: Echo-Memory: A Controlled Study of Memory in Action World Models
stories.author: Nan Duan
bullet_points: Introduces 'Echo-Memory,' a controlled study focusing on memory mechanisms within action-conditioned world models.
The research addresses a key failure point in these models: maintaining scene consistency across temporal gaps (e.g., when the camera leaves and returns).
Highlights the difficulty in comparing existing memory designs due to entangled variables in backbone, training, and evaluation methods.

stories.title: Zero Touch Predictive Orchestration: Automating Time-Series Models for the Cloud-Edge Continuum
stories.author: Raymond Knopp
bullet_points: Addresses the 'cold start' problem in orchestrating time-series models for the Cloud-Edge Continuum (CEC).
Proposes a fully automated solution to enable proactive, Zero Touch Management in volatile edge environments.
Focuses on overcoming the limitations of both localized (data-starved) and generalized (non-specific) predictive models.

stories.title: Discovering Functionally Selective Brain Regions with a Deep Topographic Multimodal Model
stories.author: Martin Schrimpf
bullet_points: Introduces 'Topo-Omni,' a new deep topographic multimodal model for neuroscience research.
Addresses limitations of previous models which were unimodal and failed to capture the contiguity of cortical processing streams.
Aims to map functionally selective brain regions by integrating visual, auditory, and language/cognitive data.

stories.title: Data Synthesis and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Low-Resource NMT: A Case Study on Q'eqchi' Mayan
stories.author: Arjun Mukherjee
bullet_points: Addresses the critical challenge of data scarcity in Neural Machine Translation (NMT) for low-resource Indigenous languages.
Proposes a novel data synthesis methodology, leveraging community-sourced dictionaries to create a synthetic corpus, thereby avoiding problematic web-scraping.
Applies Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) using LoRA adapters on mT5 for practical model bootstrapping on Q'eqchi' Mayan.

stories.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
stories.author: Lianhui Qin
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of grounding general-purpose coding agents to specialized input languages used by scientific simulators.
The core idea is to determine the minimal, simulator-specific adaptations required for an off-the-shelf coding agent to effectively control complex scientific software.
This work frames simulator setup as an agent-tool interface grounding problem, aiming to reduce the manual effort required by domain scientists.

stories.title: Data Synthesis and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Low-Resource NMT: A Case Study on Q'eqchi' Mayan
stories.author: Arjun Mukherjee
bullet_points: Addresses the critical challenge of data scarcity in Neural Machine Translation (NMT) for low-resource Indigenous languages like Q'eqchi' Mayan.
Proposes a novel data synthesis methodology, using community-sourced dictionaries to create a large synthetic corpus, avoiding problematic web-scraping.
Employs Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) via LoRA adapters on mT5 to efficiently bootstrap the NMT models.

stories.title: Multi-Turn Evaluation of Deep Research Agents Under Process-Level Feedback
stories.author: Jeff Z. Pan
bullet_points: The research addresses a gap in current benchmarks for Deep Research Agents (DRAs) by moving beyond single-shot evaluations.
It introduces a multi-turn evaluation framework to test how DRAs improve their output based on feedback.
The study compares two feedback mechanisms: internal self-reflection and external, process-level guidance targeting research strategy gaps.

stories.title: The Neutral Mask: How RLHF Provides Shallow Alignment while Leaving Partisan Structure Intact in a Large Language Model
stories.author: Wendy K. Tam
bullet_points: The paper critiques the current understanding of RLHF, suggesting it achieves only 'functional compliance' rather than deep alignment in LLMs.
It raises critical questions about the opacity of alignment training: what values are encoded, and whose values are they?
The research provides a mechanistic case study to investigate how RLHF interacts with and potentially preserves the inherent partisan structure of LLMs.

stories.title: IS-CoT: Breaking the Long-form Generation Collapse via Interleaved Structural Thinking
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Addresses the persistent challenge of long-form content generation in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Identifies a 'length collapse' issue where reasoning-enhanced models degrade sharply beyond ~2,000 words in open-ended writing.
Proposes 'IS-CoT' (Interleaved Structural Thinking) as a solution to overcome limitations in static hierarchical planning.

stories.title: Learning to Attack and Defend: Adaptive Red Teaming of Language Models via GRPO
stories.author: Mark Russinovich
bullet_points: The paper introduces AdvGRPO, a new co-training framework for adversarial red teaming of Language Models.
It addresses the instability issues of GRPO when used for joint attacker-defender optimization in AI security.
The research focuses on using reinforcement learning to enable adaptive and robust defense against evolving AI attacks.

stories.title: Your Agent Doesn't Need That 10,000-Token API Response: Context Offloading with Strands
stories.author: morganwilliscloud
bullet_points: Addresses the practical limitations of large context windows in AI agents (reliability and cost).
Introduces 'Context Offloading with Strands' as a technique to manage agent context efficiently.
Highlights the importance of 'context engineering' in building robust and cost-effective AI applications.

stories.title: Stop parsing GPT's JSON by hand: structured output with the Responses API and Zod
stories.author: dmitryvz
bullet_points: Addresses a common pain point in LLM integration: manually parsing unstructured JSON output from GPT models.
Highlights the utility of the new Responses API for ensuring structured data from AI models.
Integrates Zod for robust schema validation, improving reliability when consuming AI outputs.

stories.title: LangChain's A2A Integration: Building Multi-Agent Systems in Python Without the Cloud Lock-In
stories.author: peytongreen_dev
bullet_points: LangChain introduced langchain-adk 0.3.14, a new development.
The update focuses on enabling Agent-to-Agent (A2A) integration.
A key feature is allowing multi-agent system building in Python without cloud vendor lock-in.
